aliases.description:
Aliases that map to multiple groups. When running `fern generate <alias>`,
all groups in the alias will be run. For example:
```yaml
aliases:
  all: ["php-sdk", "ts-sdk", "go-sdk"]
```
Then `fern generate all` will run all three groups.

replay.description:
Configuration for SDK customization replay.
Automatically preserves user customizations across SDK regenerations.

autorelease.description:
If true, automatically release SDKs when changes are detected.
Can be overridden at the individual generator level.

automation.description:
Controls which automation features (GitHub Actions workflows) are enabled.
Can be overridden at the group or generator level.
All features default to true when not specified.

client-id.description: The property name for the client ID.

client-secret.description: The property name for the client secret.

scopes.description: The property name for the scopes.

access-token.description: The property name for the access token.

expires-in.description: The property name for the expires in property.

refresh-token.description: The property name for the refresh token

endpoint.description: The endpoint to get the access token, such as 'auth.get_token

refresh-token.description: The property name for the refresh token.

access-token.description: The property name for the access token.

expires-in.description: The property name for the expires in property.

refresh-token.description: The property name for the refresh token.

endpoint.description: The endpoint to refresh the access token, such as 'auth.refresh_token

token-prefix.description: Sets the token header value prefix. Defaults to 'Bearer'

token-header.description: Sets the token header key name. Defaults to 'Authorization'

placeholder.description: The placeholder value to use in code snippets (e.g., "YOUR_API_KEY").

omit.description: If true, the auth variable will be omitted from the SDK.

placeholder.description: The placeholder value to use in code snippets (e.g., "YOUR_API_KEY").

response-property.description: The property to retrieve the header value from the get token or refresh endpoint response.

header-name.description: The header name to put the token in for any authenticated HTTP request.

value-prefix.description: Commonly used for setting the `Authorization` scheme, but could be used for other things.

fernDefinition.auth.InferredAuthenticate.description: A header that will be set on HTTP requests when the inferred auth scheme is applied to an endpoint. These are not headers for the authorization endpoint.

endpoint.description: The endpoint to get the access token, such as 'auth.get_token'

expiry-response-property.description: The property name for the expiry time in the response.

authenticated-request-headers.description: The headers that will be set on HTTP requests when the inferred auth scheme is applied to an endpoint.

generators.APIDefinitionPathSchema.description: Path to the OpenAPI, AsyncAPI or Fern Definition

generators.OverridesSchema.description:
Paths to override files. Can be a single path or an array of paths.
When multiple paths are provided, overrides are applied sequentially in order.

generators.MultiServerStrategy.description: Controls how multiple top-level OpenAPI servers map to environments in the generated Fern definition.

generators.RemoveDiscriminantsFromSchema.description: Whether to remove discriminant properties from schemas when generating types, unless the schema is also used outside of a discriminated union.

generators.PathParameterOrder.description: Controls the order of path parameters in generated method signatures.

use-title.description:
Whether to use the titles of the schemas within an OpenAPI definition as the names of the types within Fern. Defaults to false.
Deprecated, use the `api.specs.[].settings.title-as-schema-name` key instead.

unions.description:
What version of union generation to use, this will grow over time. Defaults to v0.
Deprecated, use the `api.specs.[].settings.prefer-undiscriminated-unions-with-literals` key instead.

message-naming.description: What version of message naming to use for AsyncAPI messages, this will grow over time. Defaults to v1.

respect-nullable-schemas.description: Preserves nullable schemas in API definition settings. Defaults to true, where nullable schemas are treated as optional.

only-include-referenced-schemas.description: Whether to only include schemas referenced by endpoints in the generated SDK (i.e. a form of tree-shaking). Defaults to false.

inline-path-parameters.description: Whether to include path parameters within the generated in-lined request. Defaults to true.

idiomatic-request-names.description: Whether to use idiomatic request names for endpoints (e.g. ListUsersRequest instead of UsersListRequest). Defaults to true.

wrap-references-to-nullable-in-optional.description:
If true, the converter will wrap references to nullable schemas in optional.
If false, the converter will wrap references to nullable schemas in nullable.
Defaults to true.

coerce-optional-schemas-to-nullable.description:
If true, the converter will coerce nullable schemas to optional.
If false, the converter will keep nullable schemas as nullable.
Defaults to true.

group-environments-by-host.description:
If true, group servers by host into unified environments regardless of protocol.
This allows APIs with multiple protocols (REST, WebSocket, etc.) to share environment configuration.
When enabled, environment URL IDs are generated with collision resolution:
- Use server name alone if no collision
- Add path segment if collision (e.g., "prod: https://api.com/foo" -> "foo")
- Add protocol if still collision (e.g., "prod: wss://api.com/foo" -> "foo_wss", only for non-HTTPS protocols)

multi-server-strategy.description:
Controls how multiple top-level OpenAPI servers are mapped to environments in the generated
Fern definition, when those servers each declare an `x-fern-server-name` and endpoint-level
`servers:` overrides reference those names.
- `environment-per-server`: emit one environment per top-level server (each `x-fern-server-name`
  becomes its own environment constant in generated SDKs, e.g. `Environment.PRODUCTION` and
  `Environment.AGENT`). This is the default.
- `urls-per-environment`: collapse all top-level servers into a single environment with each
  server exposed as a named URL. Useful for APIs that expose multiple base hosts that together
  form a single logical environment (e.g. `api.box.com`, `upload.box.com`, `dl.boxcloud.com`).

remove-discriminants-from-schemas.description:
If `always`, remove discriminant properties from schemas when generating types, unless the schema is also used outside of a discriminated union.
If `never`, keep discriminant properties in schemas when generating types.
Defaults to `always`.

path-parameter-order.description:
Controls the order of path parameters in generated method signatures.
- `url-order`: Use the order path parameters appear in the URL path (e.g., /users/{userId}/posts/{postId})
- `spec-order`: Use the order path parameters are defined in the spec
Defaults to `url-order`.

origin.description: The URL of the API definition origin, from which the file should be polled.

overrides.description: Paths to the OpenAPI or AsyncAPI overrides. Can be a single path or an array of paths applied sequentially.

audiences.description: Audiences that you would like to filter to

target.description: The path to the target `.proto` file that defines the API (e.g. `proto/user/v1/user.proto`).

root.description: The path to the `.proto` directory root (e.g. `proto`).

overrides.description: Paths to the overrides configuration. Can be a single path or an array of paths applied sequentially.

local-generation.description: Whether to compile the `.proto` files locally. By default, we generate remotely.

from-openapi.description: Whether to convert to OpenAPI before generating

type.description: The name of the Protobuf type (e.g. google.protobuf.Struct).

id.description: The variable name as it appears in the URL template

default.description: The default value for this variable

values.description: Allowed values for this variable (from enum)

default-url.description: A separate default URL to use when no variables are provided (from x-fern-default-url extension)

url-template.description: The original URL template with variable placeholders (e.g., "https://api.{region}.example.com")

url-templates.description: URL templates with variable placeholders for each base URL (e.g., "https://api.{region}.example.com")

default-urls.description: Default URLs to use when no variables are provided (from x-fern-default-url extension)

variables.description: Server variables for URL templating, keyed by base URL ID

endpoint-security.description: Indicates that authentication requirements are defined per-endpoint via the `security` field.

generators.CoerceConstsTo.description: Controls how const values in OpenAPI specs are represented.

endpoints.description: Endpoints to include in the generated SDK (e.g. "POST /users").

max-depth.description: Controls the maximum depth for which optional properties will have examples generated. A depth of 0 means no optional properties will have examples.

except.description: Names of alias types to exclude from resolving.

respect-nullable-schemas.description: Preserves nullable schemas in API definition settings. Defaults to true, where nullable schemas are treated as optional.

title-as-schema-name.description: Whether to use the titles of the schemas within an OpenAPI definition as the names of the types within Fern. Defaults to false.

coerce-enums-to-literals.description: Whether to coerce single value enums to literals. Defaults to true.

wrap-references-to-nullable-in-optional.description:
If true, the converter will convert nullable schemas to optional nullable.
If false, the converter will convert nullable schemas to required nullable.
Defaults to true.

coerce-optional-schemas-to-nullable.description:
If true, the converter will coerce nullable schemas to optional.
If false, the converter will keep nullable schemas as nullable.
Defaults to true.

group-environments-by-host.description:
If true, group servers by host into unified environments regardless of protocol.
This allows APIs with multiple protocols (REST, WebSocket, etc.) to share environment configuration.
When enabled, environment URL IDs are generated with collision resolution:
- Use server name alone if no collision
- Add path segment if collision (e.g., "prod: https://api.com/foo" -> "foo")
- Add protocol if still collision (e.g., "prod: wss://api.com/foo" -> "foo_wss", only for non-HTTPS protocols)

multi-server-strategy.description:
Controls how multiple top-level OpenAPI servers are mapped to environments in the generated
Fern definition, when those servers each declare an `x-fern-server-name` and endpoint-level
`servers:` overrides reference those names.
- `environment-per-server`: emit one environment per top-level server (each `x-fern-server-name`
  becomes its own environment constant in generated SDKs, e.g. `Environment.PRODUCTION` and
  `Environment.AGENT`). This is the default.
- `urls-per-environment`: collapse all top-level servers into a single environment with each
  server exposed as a named URL. Useful for APIs that expose multiple base hosts that together
  form a single logical environment (e.g. `api.box.com`, `upload.box.com`, `dl.boxcloud.com`).

remove-discriminants-from-schemas.description:
If `always`, remove discriminant properties from schemas when generating types, unless the schema is also used outside of a discriminated union.
If `never`, keep discriminant properties in schemas when generating types.
Defaults to `always`.

path-parameter-order.description:
Controls the order of path parameters in generated method signatures.
- `url-order`: Use the order path parameters appear in the URL path (e.g., /users/{userId}/posts/{postId})
- `spec-order`: Use the order path parameters are defined in the spec
Defaults to `url-order`.

resolve-schema-collisions.description:
If true, resolve schema name collisions by appending numbers (e.g., Schema2, Schema3) and emit warnings.
If false, throw hard errors when schema collisions are detected.
Defaults to false.

infer-forward-compatible.description: If true, infer forward-compatible enums from oneOf [enum, string] or anyOf [enum, string] patterns. Defaults to false.

infer-default-environment.description: Whether to automatically infer the default environment from the first server. Defaults to true. When false, SDK users must explicitly provide a base URL.

coerce-consts-to.description:
Controls how `const` values in OpenAPI specs are represented.
- `literals`: Convert const values directly to literals with defaults.
- `enums`: Convert const values to single-element enums; blocks transitive coercion via `coerce-enums-to-literals`.
- `enums-coerceable-to-literals`: Convert const values to single-element enums, but allow `coerce-enums-to-literals` to coerce them transitively.
Defaults to `enums-coerceable-to-literals`.

only-include-referenced-schemas.description: Whether to only include schemas referenced by endpoints in the generated SDK (i.e. a form of tree-shaking). Defaults to false.

inline-path-parameters.description: Whether to include path parameters within the generated in-lined request. Defaults to true.

prefer-undiscriminated-unions-with-liter.description: Whether to prefer undiscriminated unions with literals. Defaults to false.

object-query-parameters.description: Enables parsing deep object query parameters

respect-readonly-schemas.description: Enables exploring readonly schemas in OpenAPI specifications

respect-forward-compatible-enums.description: Enables respecting forward compatible enums in OpenAPI specifications. Defaults to false.

use-bytes-for-binary-response.description: Enables using the `bytes` type for binary responsesin OpenAPI specifications. Defaults to a file stream.

default-form-parameter-encoding.description: The default encoding of form parameters. Defaults to JSON.

filter.description: Filter to apply to the OpenAPI specification.

additional-properties-defaults-to.description: Configure what `additionalProperties` should default to when not explicitly defined on a schema. Defaults to `false`.

type-dates-as-strings.description:
If true, convert strings with format date to strings.
If false, convert strings with format date to dates.
Defaults to true.

preserve-single-schema-oneof.description:
If true, preserve oneOf structures with a single schema.
If false, unwrap oneOf structures with a single schema.
Defaults to false.

inline-all-of-schemas.description:
Whether to inline allOf schemas. If false, allOf schemas will be
extended in the code generation.

resolve-aliases.description:
Whether to resolve aliases and inline them if possible.
If provided, all aliases will be resolved except for the ones in the except array.
Defaults to false, meaning that no aliases will be resolved.

group-multi-api-environments.description:
If true, automatically group multiple APIs with matching environments into unified environments with multiple base URLs.
This is useful for organizations with multiple APIs deployed to the same set of environments.

default-integer-format.description:
The default format to use for integer types when no format is specified in the OpenAPI schema.
Defaults to int32.

infer-discriminated-union-base-propertie.description:
If true, properties shared by every variant of a discriminated `oneOf`
(including via `allOf`/`$ref`) are lifted into the union's base properties
so SDKs can expose them directly on the union type without casting.
Defaults to false.

respect-nullable-schemas.description: Preserves nullable schemas in API definition settings. Defaults to true, where nullable schemas are treated as optional.

title-as-schema-name.description: Whether to use the titles of the schemas within an OpenAPI definition as the names of the types within Fern. Defaults to false.

coerce-enums-to-literals.description: Whether to coerce single value enums to literals. Defaults to true.

wrap-references-to-nullable-in-optional.description:
If true, the converter will convert nullable schemas to optional nullable.
If false, the converter will convert nullable schemas to required nullable.
Defaults to true.

coerce-optional-schemas-to-nullable.description:
If true, the converter will coerce nullable schemas to optional.
If false, the converter will keep nullable schemas as nullable.
Defaults to true.

- Add protocol if still collision (e.g., "prod: wss://api.com/foo" -> "foo_wss", only for non-HTTPS protocols)

message-naming.description: What version of message naming to use for AsyncAPI messages, this will grow over time. Defaults to v1.

generators.APIConfigurationV2SettingsSch.description:
Global default API import settings that apply to all specs in this API configuration.
Individual spec settings in specs[].settings will override these defaults.
Generator settings in groups.generators[].api.settings will override both.
Settings hierarchy (highest to lowest priority): generator > spec > api.

headers.description: Global Headers for the entire API

defaultEndpoint.description: If set, use this endpoint's snippet as the default whenever possible

features.description: Specifies a list of endpoints associated with the feature

customSections.description: Supply custom sections as markdown to be included in the readme

exampleStyle.description: Controls whether usage examples show only required parameters (minimal) or all parameters (comprehensive). Defaults to comprehensive.

branch.description: If specified, commits and tags the release on this branch instead of the default branch. The branch must exist.

path.description: The path to the generated snippets file.

headers.description:
Override or extend the global headers for this generator.
When provided, these headers are merged with the top-level api.headers configuration,
with per-generator headers taking precedence for same-key headers.

specs.description:
Override the specs configuration for this generator.
When provided, this completely replaces the top-level api.specs configuration.

group.GeneratorAPISettingsSchema.description:
Override API configuration settings for a specific generator.
When specs is provided, it completely replaces the top-level api.specs configuration for this generator.

generate.description:
Enable or disable SDK generation automation (fern-generate workflow).
Defaults to true.

upgrade.description:
Enable or disable CLI and generator upgrade automation (fern-upgrade workflow).
Defaults to true.

preview.description:
Enable or disable SDK preview validation on PRs (fern-preview workflow).
Defaults to true.

verify.description:
Enable or disable self-verification (install, build, test) of generated SDK output.
Runs on PRs and on merge to main.
Defaults to true.

automation.AutomationSchema.description:
Controls which automation features (GitHub Actions workflows) are enabled.
Can be set at root, group, or generator level. Resolution order:
generator → group → root → default (all enabled).

keywords.description: Overrides the keywords that require safe name variants.

snippets.description: Configures snippets for a particular generator.

ir-version.description: Overrides the version of the IR used by the generator.

smart-casing.description: Feature flag used to enable better IR naming.

api.description: Override API import settings (this is applied across all specs)

disable-examples.description: Temporary way to unblock example serialization.

publish-metadata.description: Deprecated, use `metadata` on the output block instead.

autorelease.description:
If true, automatically release this SDK when changes are detected.
Overrides the top-level autorelease setting if specified.

automation.description:
Controls which automation features are enabled for this generator.
Overrides group-level and root-level automation settings.

group.DefaultGeneratorInvocationSchema.description:
A generator invocation that uses a well-known generator name
(e.g., `fernapi/fern-typescript-sdk`) and pulls from Docker Hub.

registry.description:
The container registry URL to pull the image from during local generation.
Example: `ghcr.io/myorg` or `123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com`

generators.GeneratorImageObjectSchema.description: Explicit Docker image configuration with a custom container registry.

keywords.description: Overrides the keywords that require safe name variants.

snippets.description: Configures snippets for a particular generator.

ir-version.description: Overrides the version of the IR used by the generator.

smart-casing.description: Feature flag used to enable better IR naming.

api.description: Override API import settings (this is applied across all specs)

disable-examples.description: Temporary way to unblock example serialization.

publish-metadata.description: Deprecated, use `metadata` on the output block instead.

autorelease.description:
If true, automatically release this SDK when changes are detected.
Overrides the top-level autorelease setting if specified.

automation.description:
Controls which automation features are enabled for this generator.
Overrides group-level and root-level automation settings.

image.description: The Docker image to use for this generator, with an optional custom container registry.

group.CustomGeneratorInvocationSchema.description:
A generator invocation that specifies a custom Docker image,
optionally from a custom container registry.

group.GeneratorInvocationSchema.description:
A generator invocation. Either uses a well-known generator `name` (default)
or specifies a custom `image` with an optional container registry.

automation.description:
Controls which automation features are enabled for this group.
Overrides root-level automation settings.
Can be further overridden at the generator level.

enabled.description: Whether to enable replay for this SDK
